sourceMovie.title: The Monkey
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Monkey (2025) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Osgood Perkins's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lost Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Monkey clicked for you, The Lost Boys (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Monkey?
faq.answer: Try The Lost Boys, Braindead, Fright Night—each shares DNA with The Monkey while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Monkey?
faq.answer: The Monkey (2025) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Monkey worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
